data.title: debug-everything LOCK: 3 concurrent lock tickets created instead of 1, lock not released on session end
data.description:
## Problem

Three `[LOCK] debug-everything` task tickets were found in the ticket system (4a0d-01cf, df92-3ce0, ff10-3d15), all orphaned with no parent. The lock mechanism is designed to prevent concurrent debug-everything sessions — there should never be more than one LOCK ticket at a time.

## Expected Behavior

1. At most one `[LOCK] debug-everything` ticket should exist at any time
2. A new debug-everything session should check for an existing LOCK and refuse to start (or wait)
3. The LOCK ticket should be closed when the debug-everything session ends (via /dso:end-session or normal completion)

## Actual Behavior

3 LOCK tickets accumulated:
- 4a0d-01cf (open, no comments)
- df92-3ce0 (in_progress, 1 comment)  
- ff10-3d15 (open, no comments)

This means either:
1. The lock acquisition check does not query for existing open LOCK tickets before creating a new one
2. Multiple sessions ran concurrently without detecting each other's locks
3. Lock release on session end is not reliable (sessions end without closing their LOCK ticket)

## Scope

Investigate the debug-everything SKILL.md lock acquisition and release logic:
1. How does it check for an existing lock before creating one?
2. What happens if the session ends abnormally (SIGURG, context limit, user abort)?
3. Does /dso:end-session include LOCK ticket cleanup?
4. Should the lock use a compare-and-swap pattern (check + create atomically) rather than check-then-create?
